what_it_does: Renders a dropdown chip that lets the user pick which embedded item-store ('space') the active projection lens ranges over. It fetches the list of available spaces from the backend, hides itself when the active binding has no selectable space, and sets an explicit space override.
observations: The space list is loaded dynamically from /api/layers via fetchLayers(), then sorted alphabethetically.
space=null means 'follow the binding's default space'; selecting a space overrides it.
The chip is hidden for the 'by_nucleation' binding and for bindings whose binding.space is null.
It also hides when there is only one space available.
It uses a custom useChipMenu hook for open/close/menuClass behavior and a wrapping ref.
The menu uses framer-motion AnimatePresence with a 'enter' transition token.
Each space row gets a stamp data attribute with encodeURIComponent(sp) in the path.
Fetch failures are caught and silently ignored, causing the chip to remain hidden or empty.
standouts: Registry-driven UI: no hardcoded space list; new embedded spaces appear automatically.
Visibility is driven entirely by the active binding's self-reported space capability.
Inline comments are unusually dense and state a design philosophy ('REGISTRY-TRUE', 'UNIVERSAL projection').
conventions: React functional component with hooks (useState, useEffect).
Uses a local custom hook (useChipMenu) for chip menu behavior.
Uses stamp() for instrumentation/data attributes on interactive elements.
Uses project motion tokens via transition('enter').
concerns: The UI never offers a way to reset the override back to null/default; it only lists spaces.
Fetch errors are silently swallowed, so a broken layers endpoint leaves the control invisible without feedback.
spaces.length <= 1 hides the chip entirely, so a single-space binding never shows the current space label.
notes: A registry-driven, self-hiding lens control that exposes the engine's embedded spaces as an overridable axis.
questions: What does the layers endpoint actually return, and does it include metadata beyond the space keys?
How does the backend behave when space is set to a value the binding does not normally range over?
Is there a planned UI affordance to clear the override and return to binding.space?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: a universal, registry-true space picker that appears only when relevant.
intention_signals: The file is trying to make the projection's 'space' axis a generic, engine-described control rather than a per-lens hardcoded menu, while keeping the surface minimal by only showing it when a real choice exists.
novelty: A dynamic space selector whose options and visibility are derived from the backend's self-description of embedded spaces, with explicit override semantics over the binding default.
contribution: A reusable, registry-populated space picker that exposes the engine's embedded item-stores as a lens control, with visibility automatically gated by the active binding's reported capabilities.
summary_for_embedding: A React dropdown chip that lets the user override which embedded item-store a projection lens ranges over, populated dynamically from the engine's layer registry.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a universal, zero-hardcode operator control for the projection's space axis.

symbols.does: Renders a dropdown chip for selecting the projection's embedded space.
symbols.description: Fetches the list of embedded spaces from /api/layers, hides itself for bindings without a corpus space or with only one space, and presents a menu that overrides the binding's default space. It uses useChipMenu for menu behavior, framer-motion for animations, and stamp for instrumentation.
